5	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
6	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
7	// implement it. Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops,
8	// events, archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and
9	// are composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
10	type RawBackend interface {
11		Init() error
12		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
13		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
14		WithLock(fn func() error) error
15	}
16	
17	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
18	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
19	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
20	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
21	// JSONL export/import.
22	//
23	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
24	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
25	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
26	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
27	//
28	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
29	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
30	type Backend interface {
31		// Foundational
32		Init() error
33		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
34	
35		// CRUD
36		Create(b *Bead) error
37		Get(id string) (*Bead, error)
38		Update(id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error
39		Close(id string) error
40	
41		// Claim
42		Claim(id, assignee string) error
43		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
44		Unclaim(id string) error
45	
46		// Query
47		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
48		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
49		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)
50	
51		// Dep ops
52		DepAdd(id, depID string) error
53		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
54		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)
55	
56		// Events
57		AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
58		Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
59	
60		// Archive split
61		Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
62		Migrate() (MigrateStats, error)
63	
64		// JSONL interchange
65		Import(source, filePath string) (int, error)
66		ExportTo(w io.Writer) error
67	}

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"path/filepath"
5		"sort"
6		"sync"
7	)
8	
9	// CollectionID is the logical name of a bead-backed collection.
10	// "beads" is the active queue; future collections (e.g. "beads-archive",
11	// "exec-runs", "agent-sessions") share the same on-disk format and store
12	// API but live under their own files and locks.
13	type CollectionID string
14	
15	// CollectionSpec is the shipping description of one collection. It maps a
16	// logical name to the concrete on-disk artifacts the JSONL backend uses.
17	//
18	// Per TD-027 §(a), the registry is an in-process table seeded at startup;
19	// it is not user-editable and DDx does not auto-discover collections from
20	// disk. Additional fields from TD-027 (QueueSemantics, ArchivePartner,
21	// Attachments) are deliberately omitted at this stage — TD-027 C3 will
22	// introduce them when archive logic lands. This bead (C2) is scoped to the
23	// storage-engine refactor: registry shape plus path/lock decoupling.
24	type CollectionSpec struct {
25		// ID is the logical collection name, e.g. "beads".
26		ID CollectionID
27		// JSONLFile is the file name (not a full path) under the .ddx
28		// directory that the JSONL backend reads and writes. Empty means
29		// "<id>.jsonl".
30		JSONLFile string
31		// LockDirName is the lock directory name under the .ddx directory.
32		// Empty means "<id>.lock".
33		LockDirName string
34	}
35	
36	// FileName returns the JSONL filename for this collection, falling back to
37	// the conventional "<id>.jsonl" form when JSONLFile is unset.
38	func (c CollectionSpec) FileName() string {
39		if c.JSONLFile != "" {
40			return c.JSONLFile
41		}
42		return string(c.ID) + ".jsonl"
43	}
44	
45	// LockName returns the lock-directory name for this collection, falling
46	// back to the conventional "<id>.lock" form when LockDirName is unset.
47	func (c CollectionSpec) LockName() string {
48		if c.LockDirName != "" {
49			return c.LockDirName
50		}
51		return string(c.ID) + ".lock"
52	}
53	
54	// PathsUnder returns the absolute file and lock paths for this collection
55	// rooted at the given .ddx directory.
56	func (c CollectionSpec) PathsUnder(ddxDir string) (file, lockDir string) {
57		return filepath.Join(ddxDir, c.FileName()), filepath.Join(ddxDir, c.LockName())
58	}
59	
60	// Registry is a table of known collection specs keyed by ID. Lookups for
61	// IDs that are not registered are not an error: NewStore synthesizes a
62	// conventional spec on the fly so existing call sites that use ad-hoc
63	// collection names (e.g. test fixtures) keep working. The registry is the
64	// authoritative source for the shipping collections that DDx itself
65	// instantiates.
66	type Registry struct {
67		mu    sync.RWMutex
68		specs map[CollectionID]CollectionSpec
69	}
70	
71	// NewRegistry returns an empty registry.
72	func NewRegistry() *Registry {
73		return &Registry{specs: make(map[CollectionID]CollectionSpec)}
74	}
75	
76	// Register adds or replaces a spec.
77	func (r *Registry) Register(spec CollectionSpec) {
78		r.mu.Lock()
79		defer r.mu.Unlock()
80		r.specs[spec.ID] = spec
81	}
82	
83	// Lookup returns the spec for id and whether it was registered. Callers
84	// that want a usable spec for any name should prefer Resolve.
85	func (r *Registry) Lookup(id CollectionID) (CollectionSpec, bool) {
86		r.mu.RLock()
87		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
88		spec, ok := r.specs[id]
89		return spec, ok
90	}
91	
92	// Resolve returns the spec for id, synthesizing a conventional one (with
93	// "<id>.jsonl" / "<id>.lock") when id is not registered. This keeps the
94	// JSONL backend's per-collection layout uniform whether or not a name is
95	// in the shipping table.
96	func (r *Registry) Resolve(id CollectionID) CollectionSpec {
97		if spec, ok := r.Lookup(id); ok {
98			return spec
99		}
100		return CollectionSpec{ID: id}
101	}
102	
103	// IDs returns the registered collection IDs in lexical order.
104	func (r *Registry) IDs() []CollectionID {
105		r.mu.RLock()
106		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
107		out := make([]CollectionID, 0, len(r.specs))
108		for id := range r.specs {
109			out = append(out, id)
110		}
111		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i] < out[j] })
112		return out
113	}
114	
115	// defaultRegistry is the process-wide registry. It ships with the active
116	// "beads" collection and its archive partner "beads-archive" (TD-027 §a).
117	// Additional collections (exec-runs, agent-sessions) will be registered by
118	// their owning beads.
119	var defaultRegistry = func() *Registry {
120		r := NewRegistry()
121		r.Register(CollectionSpec{
122			ID:          DefaultCollection,
123			JSONLFile:   DefaultCollection + ".jsonl",
124			LockDirName: DefaultCollection + ".lock",
125		})
126		r.Register(CollectionSpec{
127			ID:          BeadsArchiveCollection,
128			JSONLFile:   BeadsArchiveCollection + ".jsonl",
129			LockDirName: BeadsArchiveCollection + ".lock",
130		})
131		return r
132	}()
133	
134	// DefaultRegistry returns the process-wide collection registry.
135	func DefaultRegistry() *Registry {
136		return defaultRegistry
137	}

123	// IsValidStatusTransition reports whether a bead may move from `from` to `to`
124	// under the documented state machine. Self-transitions (from == to) and
125	// transitions to/from the empty string (used by Create defaults) are rejected;
126	// callers that want to seed a fresh status should set b.Status directly before
127	// validateBead runs.
128	//
129	// The matrix below is the normative implementation of TD-031 §3 (Transition
130	// Matrix). `closed` and `cancelled` are terminal — re-opening a closed bead
131	// is not a transition; it is filing a follow-up bead with `replaces` set.
132	//
133	// Allowed transitions:
134	//
135	//	proposed    → open, cancelled
136	//	open        → in_progress, blocked, cancelled
137	//	in_progress → open, closed, blocked
138	//	blocked     → open, cancelled
139	//	closed      → (terminal)
140	//	cancelled   → (terminal)
141	func IsValidStatusTransition(from, to string) bool {
142		if from == "" || to == "" || from == to {
143			return false
144		}
145		allowed := map[string]map[string]bool{
146			StatusProposed:   {StatusOpen: true, StatusCancelled: true},
147			StatusOpen:       {StatusInProgress: true, StatusBlocked: true, StatusCancelled: true},
148			StatusInProgress: {StatusOpen: true, StatusClosed: true, StatusBlocked: true},
149			StatusBlocked:    {StatusOpen: true, StatusCancelled: true},
150			StatusClosed:     {},
151			StatusCancelled:  {},
152		}
153		if next, ok := allowed[from]; ok {
154			return next[to]
155		}
156		return false

159	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from

161	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
162	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
163	//

165	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is

168	// Allow/deny matrix:

173	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
174	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
175		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
176			return nil
177		}

194	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
195		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)

199		}

213			Status:      StatusProposed,
